activities_projects.title: Biodiversity Web Analysis
steps: Create a simple ecosystem example such as a forest
List plants, animals, and microorganisms in it
Draw connections showing how they depend on each other

activities_projects.title: Causes of Biodiversity Loss
activities_projects.objective: Identify major threats to flora and fauna
steps: List five human activities responsible for biodiversity loss
Explain how each activity affects forests and wildlife
Suggest one prevention method for each
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand environmental threats and mitigation strategies

activities_projects.title: Forest Classification Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare types of forest categories in India
steps: Create a table with Reserved, Protected, and Unclassed forests
Write definitions and characteristics of each
Add examples of states where these forests exist
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand differences in forest management types

activities_projects.title: Wildlife Protection Timeline
steps: Create a timeline of wildlife conservation in India
Include Wildlife Protection Act (1972) and Project Tiger (1973)
Add two more conservation initiatives
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand historical efforts in wildlife conservation

activities_projects.title: Mapping Tiger Reserves
activities_projects.objective: Identify major tiger reserves in India
steps: Locate five tiger reserves on the map of India
Write the state of each reserve
Mention one species protected in each reserve
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn geographic distribution of conservation areas

activities_projects.title: Community Conservation Case Study
steps: Write about a community conservation example such as Chipko Movement
Explain how local people protected forests
Describe its environmental impact
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn importance of community participation
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of case study explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sacred Groves Investigation
steps: Define sacred groves
Research one sacred grove example in India
Explain how cultural beliefs help conserve biodiversity

activities_projects.title: Joint Forest Management Study
steps: Explain what Joint Forest Management (JFM) is
Describe how communities participate in forest protection
List benefits of JFM to local communities

activities_projects.title: Endangered Species Report
steps: Choose one endangered species in India
Write about its habitat and threats
Suggest measures to protect it
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Environment Conservation Essay
steps: Write a short essay about practices that protect forests and wildlife
Include at least three examples from daily life
Suggest how students can contribute to conservation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop awareness and responsibility towards environment
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and relevance of ideas (10 marks)
